messages.author: ronan
messages.text: Just creating a new open source app for iOS, that also supports sending images. Here is the TestFlight link : https://testflight.apple.com/join/aHpCkUhM Here is the github repo : https://github.com/ronangaillard/meshcore-messenger-ios Let me know if everything works for you ! (Remember this is a really early stage version, so bug and missing features!) (edited)

messages.author: ronan
messages.text: Unfortunately, my expertise lies more with Swift and iOS development. Building a native Android app would be a significant financial strain for me right now, as I don't currently have an income. I'd need to purchase an Android phone and pay the Google Play Developer fee to publish the app. I understand the value of having a consistent experience across platforms, especially for support. I'll definitely take inspiration from Liam's app's interface and wording to make it easier for people to help each other, but I'll be careful to avoid a direct copy. I believe in creating a native feel for each platform while keeping the core user experience as consistent as possible. Thanks for the suggestions! I appreciate the feedback.
messages.content_html: Unfortunately, my expertise lies more with Swift and iOS development. Building a native Android app would be a significant financial strain for me right now, as I don't currently have an income. I'd need to purchase an Android phone and pay the Google Play Developer fee to publish the app. I understand the value of having a consistent experience across platforms, especially for support. I'll definitely take inspiration from Liam's app's interface and wording to make it easier for people to help each other, but I'll be careful to avoid a direct copy. I believe in creating a native feel for each platform while keeping the core user experience as consistent as possible. Thanks for the suggestions! I appreciate the feedback.

messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: What happens if one is in the MeshCore app and the other uses this app and sends a image for example? Does it get spammed with 40 packets with image parts in the chat?

messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: I wonder if it would be interesting to incorporate some kind of flag or capabilities indicator somehow. (in MC, not in this app per se, but just thinking out loud now) As its a shame to send a certain quantity of image part packets to someone who isnt capable or willing to receive it. (edited)
messages.content_html: I wonder if it would be interesting to incorporate some kind of flag or capabilities indicator somehow. (in MC, not in this app per se, but just thinking out loud now) As its a shame to send a certain quantity of image part packets to someone who isnt capable or willing to receive it. (edited)

messages.author: ronan
messages.text: Good question, maybe app could query receiver node to make sure it supports image receiving. But this would add two more packets to the exchange which is already packet heavy (also thinking out loud) Maybe there should a mechanism of "capabilities announcement" when announcing node that could help with that (edited)
messages.content_html: Good question, maybe app could query receiver node to make sure it supports image receiving. But this would add two more packets to the exchange which is already packet heavy (also thinking out loud) Maybe there should a mechanism of "capabilities announcement" when announcing node that could help with that (edited)
